summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud/SCM metadata to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests installer identity and cloud/SCM metadata and HTTPS POSTs it to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include: hostname, OS username, current working directory, parent project name/author/repo, git user.email parsed from ~/.gitconfig and project.git/config, GitHub CLI user from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comments (owner emails) from ~/.ssh/*.pub, recent committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, GCP project and account email from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, and the DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf. The package is brand-adjacent to Anthropic's official SDK (name `anthropic-toolkit`, description references the Anthropic SDK, peerDependency on `@anthropic-ai/sdk`), declares `main: dist/index.js` which is not shipped in the tarball, and contains no library code — its only effect on install is the data exfiltration. The data collected enables targeted follow-on attacks against the installer's cloud accounts (AWS/GCP), source control identity (GitHub), and SSH/Git infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
